discovery.run_summary: Strong risk-on tape: SPY +1.76%, QQQ +3.14%, SMH +4.38%, VIX -8% to 16.2. Drivers are a US-Iran deal framework (oil down ~3.5%, XLE -3.48%) and continued post-SpaceX-IPO digestion. Gene-editing biotechs are running hard (NTLA +23%, BEAM/CRSP up mid-single-digits) on what looks like an NTLA-led catalyst with sympathy spillover.

notable_index_moves: QQQ +3.14% and SMH +4.38% lead — semis ripping on NVDA debt-raise news and broad AI bid
XLE -3.48% on US-Iran deal framework; oil tankers/airlines beneficiaries elsewhere
VIX -8.37% to 16.2, below long-term average; SpaceX IPO digestion complete
KRE -1.61% diverges from risk-on tape — worth noting
AMR (coking coal) -5.24% — energy complex weakness bleeding into met coal despite met coal not being directly Iran-linked

discoveries.name: Intellia Therapeutics, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Small-cap biotech gapping on same-day 8-K with Reg FD + 8.01 items
discoveries.thesis: NTLA filed a 7.01 (Reg FD) + 8.01 (other events) 8-K today, and the stock is up sharply on >2x volume. The combination of Reg FD + 8.01 (not earnings, not officer change, not impairment) typically indicates a clinical data disclosure, conference presentation, or strategic update. A large gap-up on that kind of filing in a single-asset gene-editing name is the textbook response — magnitude is consistent with positive clinical/regulatory news for a binary-outcome biotech. Without reading the exhibit I cannot confirm direction of the data, but the market's verdict + sympathy bid in BEAM/CRSP suggests genuine positive news rather than overreaction.
discoveries.what_confirms: 8-K exhibit confirms positive ATTR-CM or HAE Phase 2/3 data, partnership announcement, or FDA designation; BEAM and CRSP holding sympathy gains into close
discoveries.what_kills: Exhibit reveals a dilutive offering announcement (in which case the move would reverse hard) or ambiguous data that gets re-read negatively after digestion
discoveries.what_to_learn: Same-day 8-K with items 7.01 + 8.01 (no 2.02, no 5.02) in a clinical-stage biotech is a high-signal pattern — almost always a data/regulatory/partnership disclosure timed to a conference or PR; magnitude usually reflects real news
discoveries.catalyst: Same-day 8-K (Reg FD + other events) — likely clinical data or partnership disclosure
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 7.01 Reg FD + 8.01 combination on the day of a 23% gap-up on 2.34x volume is the standard signature for a material clinical/regulatory disclosure
research_pointers: Read the 8-K exhibit directly to identify the specific program/data
Check if NTLA is presenting at an investor or medical conference this week (ASCO/ASH/EHA timing)
Compare NTA-2001 (ATTR) and NTLA-2002 (HAE) latest readouts against prior bar set by management
Confirm no concurrent equity offering filed (S-3 takedown)

discoveries.name: Beam Therapeutics Inc.
discoveries.setup: Sympathy move in gene-editing peer with no company-specific catalyst
discoveries.thesis: BEAM up ~7% on barely-elevated volume (1.19x) with no 8-K and no earnings — this is sympathy off NTLA's binary catalyst. NTLA's news (whatever it is) is company-specific, not platform-wide; base-editing (BEAM) and prime-editing are mechanistically distinct from NTLA's in vivo CRISPR programs. Sympathy bids of this magnitude in gene-editing on a peer's clinical readout typically fade within days once traders realize the readout doesn't read through to BEAM's pipeline.
discoveries.what_confirms: NTLA news turns out to be platform-validating (e.g., regulatory pathway clarification for in vivo gene editing); BEAM-specific positive news emerges
discoveries.what_kills: NTLA discloses BEAM-relevant partnership or platform read-through; BEAM holds gains on rising volume into next session
discoveries.what_to_learn: Low-volume-multiple sympathy gaps in gene-editing peers off a single-name binary catalyst typically retrace 40-60% within 3-5 sessions
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals + low volume multiple (1.19x) despite 7% gap indicates passive sympathy buying, not company-specific news flow
research_pointers: Confirm no BEAM-specific 8-K filed after market close
Check whether NTLA's disclosure has any mechanistic read-through to base-editing
Watch volume profile tomorrow — sympathy fades typically show declining volume

discoveries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
discoveries.setup: Semi-cap equipment name riding sector breakout with supporting 8-K disclosure
discoveries.thesis: AEIS +4.6% today and +21% over 5 days is consistent with the SMH +4.38% move and the broader semi-cap bid (VECO +7% today, +36% in 5 days is the more extreme version of the same trade). The June 12 8-K (items 8.01 + 9.01) likely reinforced the narrative — possibly a guidance update, contract win, or analyst day disclosure. Magnitude here is in line with sector beta times a small idiosyncratic boost; not obviously a fade or chase.
discoveries.what_confirms: 8-K exhibit shows guide raise or major customer disclosure; semi-cap equipment orders data confirms broad cycle inflection
discoveries.what_kills: 8-K is a routine disclosure (analyst day slides with no new numbers) — would suggest the move is purely sector-driven and vulnerable to SMH pullback
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 5-day +21% with semi-cap sector running hot suggests the 8-K provided fundamental support to a sector-beta trade
research_pointers: Read June 12 8-K exhibit to identify what was disclosed
Compare AEIS vs VECO/AMAT/LRCX 5-day performance to isolate idiosyncratic vs sector contribution
Check sellside estimate revisions following the filing

discoveries.name: Veeco Instruments Inc.
discoveries.setup: Extended semi-cap run with no recent 8-K and no upcoming earnings
discoveries.thesis: VECO is up ~36% in 5 days with no 8-K filings and no scheduled earnings — pure sector-momentum chase off SMH/AEIS strength and AI-capex narrative. A move of that magnitude with no company-specific disclosure in a $5B small-mid cap typically overshoots and offers an asymmetric fade once SMH consolidates. The lack of an idiosyncratic catalyst is the key tell: when fundamental news supports a 36% move, there's usually paper trail.
discoveries.what_confirms: SMH stalls or pulls back; VECO underperforms peers in the first down-tape session
discoveries.what_kills: An 8-K appears with a material contract or guide raise; HBM/advanced-packaging order data confirms a step-function demand shift specific to Veeco's tools
discoveries.what_to_learn: 5-day moves >25% in semi-cap mid-caps with zero filings or earnings nearby are momentum trades, not fundamental re-ratings — they reliably mean-revert when sector beta cools
discoveries.catalyst: Semi-cap momentum / AI capex narrative spillover
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals despite +36% in 5 days — move is sector beta amplified by short positioning, not fundamentals
research_pointers: Check short interest and days-to-cover — VECO has historically been crowded short
Confirm no pending earnings pre-announcement or analyst day
Watch SMH for trend break as primary trigger

discoveries.name: PTC Therapeutics, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Mid-cap biotech up modestly on elevated volume following a 5.02 officer-change 8-K
discoveries.thesis: PTCT filed a 5.02 (officer departure/appointment) on June 9, and the stock is up modestly today on 2.87x volume after a +8% 5-day run. 5.02 filings are bidirectional — a planned executive succession reads positive, an abrupt CFO departure mid-quarter reads negative. The price action (small positive move, large volume) is more consistent with a benign or strategic change than a crisis, but I cannot articulate either a bull or bear thesis at conf 3+ without reading the exhibit.
discoveries.what_confirms: Exhibit shows a strategic new hire (CEO/CMO upgrade) — bullish read-through
discoveries.what_kills: Exhibit shows unplanned CFO or CMO departure with no replacement — would invert the read
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 2.87x volume on a low-magnitude price move following a 5.02 filing suggests institutional re-pricing of management quality, direction not determinable from filing code alone
research_pointers: Read the 5.02 exhibit to identify role and circumstances
Check if departure/appointment relates to commercial leadership for upcoming launches
Cross-reference with sellside notes from June 9-10

discoveries.name: Alpha Metallurgical Resources
discoveries.setup: Met coal name selling off on energy-complex sympathy despite no direct Iran exposure
discoveries.thesis: AMR -5.24% today and -5.89% over 5 days as XLE -3.48% on the US-Iran deal — but AMR is coking coal for steel, not thermal coal or oil. The fundamental linkage to Iran/Hormuz oil supply is weak; met coal pricing tracks Chinese steel demand and Australian export tonnages, not Brent. Below-average volume (0.77x) on the selloff suggests passive ETF/energy-complex outflows rather than dedicated fundamental selling. Setup for a relative-value bounce if energy stabilizes.
discoveries.what_confirms: Thermal coal and oil stabilize while AMR continues lower (would indicate met coal weakness is fundamental, not sympathy) — wait, that kills it. Confirms: oil bounces while AMR lags behind on the recovery, or Chinese steel PMI prints firm and AMR catches a relative bid
discoveries.what_kills: Hard coking coal benchmark prices roll over; Chinese steel demand prints weak; AMR-specific operational issue surfaces
discoveries.what_to_learn: Energy-complex selloffs frequently drag met coal names on ETF mechanics despite no fundamental linkage — sub-1x volume on the selloff is the tell
discoveries.catalyst: Sympathy with XLE / oil weakness on US-Iran deal framework
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals + below-average volume on a 5%+ down move during XLE -3.48% session points to passive energy-complex selling, not idiosyncratic news
research_pointers: Pull current Australian hard coking coal benchmark price (PLV FOB) — primary AMR driver
Check Chinese steel rebar futures and crude steel output data
Confirm no operational issue at AMR's Central App mines

catalyst_chains.primary_event: NTLA gap-up on same-day 8-K (likely clinical/regulatory disclosure)
US-Iran deal framework — oil down sharply, Strait of Hormuz reopening

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump claims Iran peace deal, ships moving through Strait of Hormuz, oil expected to flow both directions after Friday signing
trump_signals.note: Already largely priced (XLE -3.48%, oil tumbling, VIX -8%). Watch for follow-through if Friday signing actually occurs vs fade if details unravel — Hormuz management terms still disputed per CNBC. Sympathy moves in non-oil energy names (coal, refiners) may have overshot.

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump threatens 100% wine tariffs on France unless French tech sales tax is scrapped, ahead of G7
trump_signals.note: Wine import names face headline risk into G7 summit. Market is not pricing this — discoveries today don't include any wine/spirits names, but worth monitoring for setup.

ai_announcements.headline: Salesforce acquires AI customer service platform Fin for $3.6B
ai_announcements.summary: Salesforce buys Fin to bolster Agentforce, its AI agent platform for enterprise customer service automation.
ai_announcements.note: No customer-service-pure-play public stocks in the moved list (no ZEN/ASAP/FIVN/etc.). PRDO down 5% is education sector, unrelated to this M&A.

ai_announcements.headline: Cybersecurity vets protest US government export-control ban on Anthropic's Fable and Mythos models
ai_announcements.summary: Cybersecurity experts pushing White House to remove export restrictions on top Anthropic models, arguing it hurts defensive cyber work. This is an Anthropic-related call — flagging for bias review. Net read: it confirms USG views Fable/Mythos as frontier-grade (capability signal) but also constrains Anthropic's international distribution. No clean public-stock read among today's movers.

ai_announcements.headline: Meta rolls out 'AI Mode' on Facebook using cross-platform public data
ai_announcements.summary: Meta adding generative AI features to Facebook feed/search. Incremental, not a competitive shock to any name in the movers list.

ai_announcements.headline: NewCore raises $66M for AI agent identity management
ai_announcements.summary: Identity layer for AI agents — adjacent to OKTA/CYBR/SAIL but none appear in today's movers.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Today's biggest movers (NTLA +23%, VECO +7%, BEAM +7%, CRSP +5.6%, AEIS +4.6%, PTCT +2%, PRDO -5%, AMR -5%) do not have a clear mechanistic link to today's AI announcements. NTLA/BEAM/CRSP/PTCT moves are gene-editing/biotech sector action (likely NTLA-specific 8-K driving sympathy in CRISPR names — not AI-driven). VECO/AEIS are semicap equipment continuing a multi-day rally (+36% and +21% on 5-day) — possibly broader AI capex tape but no specific catalyst in today's news. PRDO (for-profit education) decline could loosely tie to ongoing AI-displaces-education narrative but no specific announcement today justifies a call. AMR (coking coal) is unrelated. No high-confidence AI-driven mispricing setups in this batch.
